medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Mr. ___ is a ___ year old man with a hx of stage I melanoma s/p resection, prostate cancer s/p prostatectomy and recently found to have NSCLC with right sided lung mass, hilar and mediastinal nodes positive and a right pleural effusion. He presented to the emergency room with worsening dyspnea this evening. Over the course of the past several days, his oxygen saturation has gradually decreased by 1-2%. He slowly became more short of breath with exertion such as walking out of the house. Denies worsening of cough but has a non-productive cough. He has felt progressively weak. Initial vitals: 99.1 94 86/57 20 91%. CXR showed worsening R moderate pleural effusion and suspected L sided pleural effusion.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Diabetes mellitus (last A1c 6.6 in ___ diet controlled), BPPV, Melanoma (Stage I, dx and excised in ___), Prostate cancer s/p radical prostatectomy and negative LN dissection (___), Cataracts, Pulmonary nodules (first noted in ___, s/p biopsy; followed by Dr. ___ at ___), Neuropathy, Tremor, Diverticulosis, Colonic polyps (adenomatous in ___), Celiac artery aneurysm (followed by Dr. ___ at ___) s/p CCY s/p inguinal hernia repair.
medical_history.family_history: No family history of clots or bleeding disorders. No h/o CAD, DM, colon or prostate cancer.

physical_examination.general_examination: VS: T98.3, BP 124/66, HR 94, RR 20, 94% on 4L. General: alert and oriented, breathing comfortably, NAD. LUNGS: Decreased breath sounds at the bases bilaterally, right greater than left, with dullness on percussion. RLE trace edema, RLE is larger than LLE.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological: A&Ox3. Appropriate. CN ___ grossly intact. Preserved sensation throughout. ___ strength throughout.
